segments.text: I recently did a video answering the question,\h does a VPN really protect privacy? We received\h\h
lots of comments on this and I read them. So, I\h thought it would be a good idea to do a follow-up\h\h
video and correct one error that I made and\h clarify some other points. A lot of people\h\h
asked a lot of good questions. So, let's cover\h those. Just to be clear, in this video, I'm going\h\h
to focus strictly on personal VPNs, whereas the\h original video also touched on corporate VPNs.\h\h
So just bear that in mind. Okay. So here goes.\h Let's start off with a quick review of what a VPN\h\h
is. A virtual private network. And then in that\h I'll include the correction that I need to make\h\h
with my previous description. So what happens\h is we've got a user here with a browser wants\h\h
to hit a website. So they send their information\h over. Typically most websites these days do this\h\h
protocol called HTTPS. If you see the S, then it's\h supposed to mean secure. Meaning the information\h\h
is encrypted. So it's encrypted here and decrypted\h on this side. So what we're trying to guard\h\h
against with that is that as your information goes\h across the internet, not everyone on the internet\h\h
can see what your traffic is. So that's the idea\h with HTTPS. And that's particularly important if\h\h
you're sending passwords and things like that.\h You wouldn't want those being exposed. But it\h\h
doesn't guard against everything. For instance,\h you've got an internet service provider who gave\h\h
you this IP in the first place and gave you an\h on-ramp to the internet. They will still be able\h\h
to see your traffic. They will be able to see\h the IP address that you're going to and how often\h\h
you go there. So, if that's a concern, then you\h might want an additional level of privacy. Now,\h\h
why would you care about the ISP? Well, your ISP\h could have a data breach, and if they did, then\h\h
all the records that they've kept about your web\h surfing habits could become public information,\h\h
and maybe you don't want that. It could also\h be the case that uh through a court order,\h\h
law enforcement contacts the ISP and say, "We\h want to see all the web traffic this person has\h\h
done." Well, then maybe you you don't want that\h to be the case. So, if you decide that you would\h\h
rather not do that, then uh you can do that. ISPs\h also might use your information and sell that. So,\h\h
they're using your web traffic as a way to market\h information about you to others and therefore they\h\h
profit and maybe you don't want them to do that.\h So, there's a number of different reasons why you\h\h
don't necessarily want your ISP knowing everywhere\h you go all the time. And if that's the case,\h\h
that's when you would bring in a virtual private\h network. Here we have a separate provider. And\h\h
what we're going to do in this case is we're\h going to actually set up a separate session.\h\h
So this user, and I'm showing this as a pipe\h or a tunnel because we're going to tunnel this,\h\h
we're going to install a special client here,\h piece of software that they're going to use. And\h\h
with that client, they're going to encrypt all\h the information that goes out of that client.\h\h
And where it all goes is it all goes right here.\h So, and here I'm going to have a decryption point\h\h
where all the information gets decrypted. So, it\h can travel across this public network, but people\h\h
won't be able to see what it is. Just like with\h HTTPS, the difference is the ISP doesn't see where\h\h
you're going. All they see is you keep going to\h this VPN provider again and again and again. So,\h\h
they're basically blinded to what's happening\h here. Now, what happens is you're still running\h\h
an HTTP session. You're going to run it directly\h through the VPN provider. And this is the part I\h\h
need to correct because in the previous video I\h talked about the VPN provider could also set up\h\h
a separate session and encrypt that information\h with a separate tunnel. They could and that's\h\h
basically a proxy configuration. Most of them\h don't do that. So I don't want to be misleading\h\h
and let's show a more accurate more commonplace\h example of this. So we still have the end-to-end\h\h
encryption between the website and the user which\h is all good but now we have it so that the ISP is\h\h
kind of taken out of the loop to a great extent.\h They just see everything going to one place and\h\h
then they don't see more. Now what that means is\h do you have anonymity? Well no not really because\h\h
this this website well they know who you are.\h If you logged into them then they know who you\h\h
are. They also might set cookies in your browser\h that allow them to track your habits and where\h\h
you're going. Do they know where you're coming\h from, your IP address? Well, no, because all\h\h
they're going to see is the IP address for the\h VPN provider for every time you come through,\h\h
no matter where you happen to be, they're going\h to see the IP address of that. Do they know your\h\h
geographic location? No. They're going to\h see whatever the geographic location of the\h\h
VPN provider is. So that way you're getting some\h level of privacy protection also from the website\h\h
itself by using the VPN. And how does all of that\h happen? Well, there's a thing to understand here.\h\h
When you send information over the internet,\h there's there's a a a packet that you're going\h\h
to send. And with one of those packets, it's going\h to look like this. We've got basically a portion\h\h
that is known as the header and a portion that is\h known as the payload. And the payload is really\h\h
all your data. That's the stuff you're sending to\h the website or the website is sending back to you.\h\h
That's the stuff that you really most care about.\h But up here in this header, we're going to include\h\h
a lot of information. Most importantly is a source\h IP address and a destination IP address. So your\h\h
source address would be you. The destination\h would be where you're trying to get to. That's\h\h
going to be included in there. And that's the\h information that even if you have HTTPS turned on,\h\h
that information is still exposed. So you're going\h to encrypt with HTTPS the payload. So that's all\h\h
the sensitive information you might be sending\h to that web server, your passwords, all that kind\h\h
of stuff. But if you're doing this scenario, your\h source address and destination address along with\h\h
a few other things like that are still in the\h clear. They have to be in the clear. Otherwise,\h\h
we wouldn't know how to route your traffic\h around. So, by doing it this way, all of this\h\h
information gets protected. Now, uh the ISP still\h sees a source and destination address. It's just\h\h
what they see as a destination address is always\h this and the source address is you. And of course,\h\h
they knew that anyway. So that's a key distinction\h is looking at now we've covered this correction\h\h
so that we don't have a second tunnel and now\h hopefully you understand the difference between\h\h
the header and the payload in the packet and which\h part is protected by HTTPS and which part is not.\h\h
Another reason you might want to use a VPN is\h because it lets you bypass certain controls or\h\h
restrictions. So if we've got some restrictions\h what what might these be? Well, one might be\h\h
content restriction. So, if you live in a country\h where there are rules on what you can see, limits\h\h
on free speech, uh, news reports you're allowed to\h access and not things like that, someone might use\h\h
a VPN to hide their address because one of the\h things that happens here also is geographical\h\h
restrictions can be removed because if the VPN is\h the address that the website sees and that VPN is\h\h
in the the safe zone, well, then you can basically pick a VPN provider to appear anywhere in\h\h
the world. So if you need to appear that you're\h coming from a different country that doesn't have\h\h
those restrictions, you pick a VPN provider that\h has a server in that area. And then that way the\h\h
geographical content limitations, censorship, any\h sort of inspection of the content you're sending,\h\h
again, will not be able to be easily seen. And\h then there may be, like I said, certain sites\h\h
that could be blocked. uh it could be certain\h uh content that you've paid for but uh you are\h\h
not a able to access that when you're outside\h of your home country and you feel like maybe\h\h
you still should. So a lot of people use them in\h that way. It's uh in some cases it's for privacy,\h\h
some cases it's just for access. So another thing\h and this is really the point of the previous\h\h
video that I want to underline uh here again is\h that a VPN is essentially a transfer of trust.\h\h
It does not take away all privacy issues. We'll\h talk about anonymity a little more in a minute,\h\h
but what it does is it transfers trust. You, your\h VPN provider may in fact be keeping a log of all\h\h
the traffic that comes in just like your ISP might\h have been doing. So now instead of relying on this\h\h
database that the ISP is keeping that's not going\h to see much of interest, all of the interesting\h\h
stuff is going to be in this database. So now\h it depends on how much can I trust these guys.\h\h
Are these guys ones who are going to turn this\h information over readily? Maybe they're compelled\h\h
to by a court order, in which case they have to.\h Maybe they sell your information. You thought\h\h
you were getting away from that with the ISP. But\h this VPN, by the way, if you're not paying for it,\h\h
then you are the product, not the customer. So if\h you got a free VPN, they are probably monetizing\h\h
your data and you think you are getting\h something that's more private. In fact,\h\h
you're just concentrating all your information\h to one place where they can sell it or abuse\h\h
your information. So it all depends and these guys\h could also get hacked. So there nobody's perfect.\h\h
So you're transferring your trust from the ISP\h about some of this information to a VPN provider.\h\h
And if they do a good job, then you have improved\h your privacy. If they don't do a good job,\h\h
you haven't. So, you may be wondering about this\h thing called Tor, the onion router. Some people\h\h
asked about that in the comments, and you might\h say, well, why wouldn't I use that? What are the\h\h
pros and cons? Let's take a look at that. First\h of all, what is tor? Let's look at that a little\h\h
bit. Well, with tor, if we're the user here and\h the website we're trying to get to over here,\h\h
so the same scenario, the user is going to route\h their traffic through a tor node, a a relay. And\h\h
this particular node then will route their traffic\h to another relay node that is in the tor network\h\h
which will then route it to yet another. And then\h ultimately after it's done three hops then it's\h\h
going to send your information to the web server.\h So that's now I drew that in a different color\h\h
because this information is actually not encrypted\h as part of the tor network. Each one of these are\h\h
encrypted connections and the advantage to this\h is no single to node if it were compromised would\h\h
know the whole story and be able to reconstruct\h everything. So you're basically splitting the\h\h
the responsibility up and therefore you're not\h putting all your eggs in one basket. That's the\h\h
the thought. But notice again this part is not\h protected. So you you would need to use either\h\h
uh maybe HTTPS connection or something like that\h in order to secure that last bit of it. But let's\h\h
take a look at a comparison of the two. So if\h we've got VPN, which we've been talking about\h\h
in this video, and we've got tor, um what are\h the advantages and disadvantages of each of them?\h\h
Well, in terms of protection from your ISP, well,\h they're both going to give you that. So you've got\h\h
the ability now so that your ISP, if you're using\h a VPN or tor, the ISP is just going to see you're\h\h
entering the VPN or you're entering the tour\h network. That's really all they're going to see.\h\h
How about anonymity? Well, I mentioned earlier\h that the VPN uh gives you sort of anonymity, but\h\h
not so much because uh it's it's not protecting\h all of the information all through the entire the\h\h
entire path. Um, tor is something that would be\h much stronger for anonymity if that's what you're\h\h
really after. And this is really the strong\h suit of tor. This is where it's really going\h\h
to to have an advantage over a VPN is it will do a\h much better job of maintaining your anonymity. Uh,\h\h
the again with the VPN, the VPN provider knows\h who you are and could track you back. The tor\h\h
network would have a difficulty tracking you back\h because of all the hops it's gone through. Now,\h\h
another thing though that we might look at\h is speed. Okay, this is why you may want to\h\h
reconsider if you think a VPN would be the answer\h or a tour would be the answer. A VPN is relatively\h\h
fast. Now, it's going to be slower than if you\h were just going directly to the website because\h\h
it's adding an extra hop. You've got to traverse\h this server and you're going to encrypt your\h\h
information. But what's not going to be good for\h tour is this speed. It's got to go through three\h\h
different hops with different encryptions. This\h in fact leads to the next one and that's related\h\h
to streaming. So if what you're doing is streaming\h say a video online, okay, a VPN could do a decent\h\h
job of that. Tor probably not not unless you like\h watch watching lots of buffering messages. Not\h\h
going to be such a a great thing for that.\h Um, another one to consider is simplicity.\h\h
So, how simple is this thing to deal with? Well,\h a VPN is relatively simple. It's not as simple\h\h
as just going straight to the website, but you\h install the client software, you log into it,\h\h
you turn the VPN on, and you pretty much set it\h and forget it in most cases. However, tor is a\h\h
much more complicated thing to deal with. I'm not\h even going to try to get into the the details of\h\h
how you set this up, but if you were trying to\h get to the dark web, you'd use a tor browser.\h\h
But if you're trying to get to the dark web, well\h then you're going to know more details presumably\h\h
before you try doing something like that. Another\h use case to consider if you're on public Wi-Fi. In\h\h
this case, if you're trying to protect, let's\h say you've gone to the coffee shop and you've\h\h
connected into their Wi-Fi and you want to make\h sure that that the Wi-Fi in in the the coffee shop\h\h
can't see all the places that you're going to.\h Well, a VPN uh could do a good job of that. If\h\h
you were using tor for that, uh yeah, it could\h do it, but this is really overkill. I mean, that\h\h
that's a lot of of heavy lifting uh for a case\h like that. And then the last one I'm going to do\h\h
here is a whistleblower. So if we've got someone\h who is and a whistleblower is the idea that you're\h\h
trying to to tell the world about something but\h still maintain your privacy. You need to let the\h\h
world know about some abuse that's happening in\h a company or in a country or something like that.\h\h
So you're blowing the whistle on that. Well,\h whistleblowing um maybe not so much for a VPN.\h\h
It's not going to protect everything. Again, the\h VPN could be audited, could be compelled to hand\h\h
over the records. So maybe not the best case. This\h is where tor is going to give you an advantage.\h\h
So you can see there's pros and cons to each\h one of these technologies and there's the right\h\h
use case for one and the right set of use cases\h for the other. As you can see, once you go down\h\h
the VPN rabbit hole, things aren't always what\h they seem. Do VPNs really protect privacy? Well,\h\h
that's a definite maybe. It all depends on\h what you're trying to protect and who you're\h\h
transferring your trust to. Just remember, if\h you aren't paying for it, you're the product,\h\h
not the customer. That means a free VPN probably\h monetizing the info it gathers about you and your\h\h
traffic. Just because you paid though, doesn't\h necessarily mean it's better. So, which VPN is\h\h
best? Well, we don't do product reviews on this\h channel, so I'm not going to be telling you which\h\h
one to use. But I will recommend that you check\h out the articles and videos across the internet\h\h
and there are many from those who have done these\h reviews. Then decide which one is right for you.
